sections.recommendation:
Refer to the City Manager to direct the Departments of Public Works, Police and Fire to
work collaboratively to implement Safe Passages Program – EMBER Initiative Phase 1
and 2 and report back to City Council on progress no later than the 2nd week of May
2025. This program will further facilitate responder ingress and community evacuation
during wildfires by developing a plan to create and enforce no-parking zones in addition
to existing 24/7/365 red curbs and no-parking zones during extreme fire weather
conditions and/or the broader fire season.
FINANCIAL IMPLICATIONS
If these actions cannot be implemented within the budgets of the involved departments,
the City Manager will return to Council with a report demonstrating the funding required
to complete each phase.
sections.current_situation:
The recent catastrophic fires in Los Angeles are a sobering reminder of the risk to
wildfire that we face here in Berkeley. The parallels these southern California
communities share with Berkeley in topography, population density, road configuration,
hazardous vegetation fuel loading, proximity to wildland interfaces should be sufficient
to spur us to act now to try to make our community more resistant to fire and safer for
everyone.
Anyone who has driven in the Berkeley hills knows that parking on narrow streets can
impair emergency vehicle access as well as emergency evacuation. The Safe
Passages project aims to build on past efforts to mitigate risks by supporting Berkeley’s
Emergency Evacuation Plan and help to ensure we have safe ingress routes for first
responders and safe egress routes for community members.
sections.background:
Safe Passages is integrated into the suite of proposals in the 2025 Effective Wildfire
Mitigation Measures for Berkeley’s EMBER Resilience (EBMER) initiative:
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7160 ● TDD: (510) 981-6903 ● Fax: (510) 981-7166
E-Mail: Bblackaby@berkeleyca.gov
Page 2 of 4
Request Inter-Departmental Collaboration and Implementation of Safe Passages ACTION CALENDAR
February 11, 2025
Phase 1
Staff will verify and install the appropriate signage and markings in areas with existing
red curbs to enable enforcement and towing of illegally parked vehicles:
 In areas of the city most vulnerable to wildfire, staff will evaluate existing no
parking areas for painted red curbs and appropriate signage
 Within the existing budget, staff will update signage to enable towing and paint
curbs red
 Staff will develop a cost estimate for painting and installation of additional
signage if it cannot be accomplished within the existing budget
Phase 2
Staff will return to Council with an analysis of options and any associated costs to
implement programs, policies and laws that would further reduce congestion of streets
and offer a proposal for a more wide-spread parking restriction program in the City’s
Very High Hazard Fire Severity Zone. This should include but is not limited to:
 Designation (with signs, paint or other means) additional critical pinch points and
roadway segments where on-street parking will hinder evacuation and responder
ingress during a wildfire scenario,
 Potential incentives for homeowners to clear off-street parking such as permit fee
reductions for paving new off-street parking areas, or removing clutter in garages
to allow parking,
 A program similar to Los Angeles that designates parking restrictions in certain
additional areas during the fire season or specifically during periods of
heightened risk like Red Flag Weather.
As a reference for how many days there has been Red Flag Weather declared in
Berkeley, see the table
